options.label: A
options.text: Voltage is the amount of charge flowing past a point each second.

options.label: B
options.text: Voltage is the energy per unit charge that pushes electrons around a circuit.

options.label: C
options.text: Voltage is the resistance to the flow of electrons in a wire.

options.label: D
options.text: Voltage is the time it takes for electrons to travel through a conductor.

explanation: Voltage (measured in volts) represents the energy that pushes electrons around the circuit. It is the 'push' or 'pressure' that drives current through a circuit. Option A describes current, option C describes resistance, and option D describes time—none of these are voltage.
